decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to combo off with Balustrade Spy in order to mill your entire library and put all your creatures into the graveyard. This allows you to potentially win the game on the same turn using cards like Dread Return to bring back powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant for a game-winning burst of damage. The deck includes a lot of mana ramp and card draw to help you assemble your combo pieces quickly and efficiently. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with a big board presence and powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on using artifacts, particularly creatures like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar, to quickly play cheap spells such as Galvanic Blast and Thoughtcast to deal damage efficiently. Synergies with cards like Makeshift Munitions and Nihil Spellbomb help to control the board and enhance the aggro strategy, while also utilizing cards like Ichor Wellspring for card draw. The land base includes artifact lands like Vault of Whispers to support affinity synergies. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast-paced aggressive strategy in the Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

The main strategy of this deck is to control the game by utilizing cheap spells and creatures to slow down the opponent while generating card advantage through card draw and recursion. The key focus is on consistently maintaining board presence and out-valuing the opponent by disrupting their game plan and stalling until the control player can assemble their win condition. The inclusion of Ephemerate allows for additional value from flickering key creatures or spells for additional benefits, adding to the control player's advantage over time.

This Pauper control deck focuses on utilizing Gates to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck includes a mix of counter spells, removal spells, and card draw to maintain control of the game. By using Citadel Gate and Heap Gate, the deck can generate mana advantage while deploying threats like Outlaw Medic and Guardian of the Guildpact. The deck also includes a strong defensive package with cards like Prismatic Strands and Journey to Nowhere. Overall, the deck aims to out-value and outlast the opponent in a game of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to outmaneuver and outlast the opponent. The deck also utilizes creatures like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic to apply pressure on the opponent while maintaining control. The inclusion of cards like Mental Note, Thought Scour, and Brainstorm helps to filter through the deck and find key cards needed to disrupt the opponent's strategies. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win through attrition.

The main idea behind this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of Gates and creatures with abilities that enhance their strength and resilience. The Gates provide mana fixing and card advantage, while cards like Malevolent Rumble and Journey to Nowhere help maintain board control. Outlaw Medic and Guardian of the Guildpact serve as solid defensive options, while Armadillo Cloak provides a powerful boost to your creatures. This deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence to ultimately outlast and outvalue them.

The main concept of this Pauper Jund Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells while utilizing land destruction and graveyard hate to control the board. By combining efficient threats with removal and graveyard disruption, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building its own board presence and advancing towards victory. The deck's synergy and versatility allow it to adapt to different matchups and strategies, making it a strong contender in the Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures and burn spells. By efficiently utilizing cards like Voldaren Epicure, Sneaky Snacker, Kessig Flamebreather, Fireblast, Lava Dart, Fiery Temper, and Lightning Bolt, this deck aims to apply pressure early on and finish the game with direct damage spells. Guttersnipe and Grab the Prize provide additional reach and card advantage, while Faithless Looting helps filter through the deck for key cards. Snow-Covered Mountains are used to ensure consistency in mana base.

The deck focuses on rapidly deploying artifact creatures and utilizing various artifact synergies to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and efficient removal. By leveraging card draw effects and sacrificing artifacts, the deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's plans. With the ability to quickly generate large amounts of damage through direct damage spells and creature attacks, the deck looks to pressure the opponent and close out games before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap creatures and sacrificing them to generate value, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Cleansing Wildfire. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature aggression and removal spells, while also having some card draw and mana fixing options to ensure consistency in gameplay. The deck's color combination allows for a versatile playstyle, making it adaptable to different matchups and strategies.

The deck focuses on utilizing sacrifice effects to generate value from artifacts and creatures, while simultaneously disrupting the opponent's strategy with land destruction and graveyard hate. By sacrificing artifacts and creatures, the deck can gain card advantage and power up cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Gixian Infiltrator. The aggressive nature of the deck is supported by efficient removal spells like Cast Down and the ability to close out games quickly with cards like Lembas and Refurbished Familiar. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with threats and disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells, sacrifice effects, and card draw. Utilizing cards like Cast Down, Defile, and Snuff Out to deal with threats on the board, while also generating value with cards like Ichor Wellspring and Thorn of the Black Rose. The deck also aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with graveyard hate from cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Bojuka Bog. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of controlling elements and synergistic card choices.

The focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing spells such as Counterspell, Dispel, and Skred to disrupt your opponent's strategy. The key strategy is to leverage the powerful card advantage provided by Ephemerate to repeatedly blink value creatures like Mulldrifter and Augur of Bolas. This allows you to draw cards, control the board, and outvalue your opponent over time. Additionally, the deck includes removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Thraben Charm to deal with threats, while also including utility spells like Late to Dinner and Union of the Third Path for added flexibility.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent using burn spells and creatures with abilities that trigger whenever you cast an instant or sorcery spell. By efficiently playing low-cost spells and creatures, you aim to overwhelm your opponent quickly before they can set up their defenses or stabilize the board. The deck relies on dealing direct damage to the opponent while also utilizing cards like Grab the Prize and Faithless Looting for card advantage and resource management.

The deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent using cheap aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan with direct damage and lifegain effects, while also generating card advantage through looting effects. The deck aims to outpace the opponent by maximizing its aggressive potential and utilizing efficient removal spells to control the board. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with quick, consistent damage output and efficient resource management.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt your opponent's game plan through counterspells and removal spells, while also utilizing card draw and graveyard manipulation to maintain card advantage and progress towards finishing the game with powerful threats like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic. The deck also includes a mix of utility spells and lands to help control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. By controlling the game and outlasting the opponent's resources, this deck aims to secure victory through careful planning and strategic decision-making.
